title: Mincha: Why the Afternoon Prayer Is Considered the Hardest — and Most Powerful
title_zh: 午后祷告:为什么下午祷告最难——也最有力量
title_he: מנחה: למה תפילת אחר הצהריים נחשבת הקשה ביותר — והעוצמתית ביותר
description: The challenge of stopping mid-day to pray, why the Talmud says Elijah was answered specifically at Mincha, and tips for consistency.
key_concepts: Chinese cultural parallels (午时, 未时, 氣)

chinese_historical_context.chinese_equivalent: Republic of China period (民國時期)

author: Torah Light Editorial Team
target_audience: Chinese native Mandarin speakers with no prior Jewish knowledge
cultural_bridges: Wu shi and Wei shi (午时, 未时) - Chinese traditional afternoon rest times
Qi (氣) energy flow in Chinese medicine
Comparison of continuous work vs. necessary pauses in both traditions
